Demand trend

System demand, on a 5-minute average, compared to the forecasted demand.

Chart data (CSV)

Chart

Combination chart with 4 data series.
The chart has 1 X axis displaying categories.
The chart has 1 Y axis displaying MW. Data ranges from 15301 to 25961.
End of interactive chart.

Net demand trend

System demand minus wind and solar, on a 5-minute average, compared to system and forecasted demand.

Chart data (CSV)

Chart

Combination chart with 5 data series.
The chart has 1 X axis displaying categories.
The chart has 1 Y axis displaying MW. Data ranges from -7875 to 25557.
End of interactive chart.

Resource adequacy capacity trend

Resource adequacy (RA) is capacity designated by the state to be bid into the market for the reliable operation of the power grid, minus the impacts of outage derates. Any capacity needed over that designated amount has to be procured in the real-time market.

Chart data (CSV)

Chart

Chart with 18 data series.
The chart has 1 X axis displaying Time. Data ranges from 2026-04-03 00:00:00 to 2026-04-04 00:00:00.
The chart has 1 Y axis displaying MW. Data ranges from -4710 to 53464.
End of interactive chart.
*Note: Values for the 7th day of the day-ahead forecasts will complete by 9:30 PT.

7-day resource adequacy capacity trend

Resource adequacy capacity forecast for today plus the next 7 days, compared to demand forecast plus reserve requirements.

Chart data (CSV)

Chart

Chart with 18 data series.
The chart has 1 X axis displaying Time. Data ranges from 2026-04-03 00:00:00 to 2026-04-11 00:00:00.
The chart has 1 Y axis displaying MW. Data ranges from -5888 to 55795.
End of interactive chart.
*Note: Values for the 7th day of the day-ahead forecasts will complete by 9:30 PT.

Today’s Outlook charts are designed to summarize forecasts and actual loads. The demand and net demand trend data do not include dispatchable pump loads or battery storage that is charging on the system. This data is for informational purposes only, and should not be used for determining actual billing values or operational planning.

Data is subject to change without notice.
For official data, visit OASIS. For official emissions data, visit CARB.

Demand information
Demand

The amount of power that is needed on the grid.

Current demand 1-minute maximum
The amount of power currently needed on the grid.
Available capacity in next hour*
Total of all resources’ maximum available MW outputs, minus current outputs. Includes spin, non-spin, and available regulation up reserves. Includes all resource types except solar, wind, demand response, and battery storage providing reserves.
Calculated every 15 minutes based on Real-Time Pre-Dispatch (RTPD).
Available capacity in next 4 hours*
Total of all resources’ maximum available MW outputs, minus current outputs. Includes the available capacity in the next hour and remaining battery storage ,assuming 100% state-of-charge (SOC).
Calculated every 60 minutes based on Short-Term Unit Commitment (STUC).
Reserves 5-minute interval
Total of all spin, non-spin, and available regulation up, including battery storage providing reserves. Included in available capacity in next hour. Calculated based on Real-Time Pre-Dispatch reserve requirements.
Historical peak
52,061 MW (Sep 6, 2022)
Maximum amount of power recorded since ISO inception in 1998. View historical peaks.
*Capacity varies due to startup, constraints, outages, congestion, and emergencies. Does not include solar and wind or Demand Response resources.
Forecasted demand

Power expected to be needed during the day, based on historical system loads and temperatures. The day-ahead forecast is determined 24 hours before the power is needed. The hour-ahead forecast is determined an hour before the power is needed.

Forecasted peak 5-minute interval
Maximum amount of power expected during the day.
Tomorrow’s forecasted peak 1-hour interval
Maximum amount of power expected tomorrow.
Demand trend information
Demand
Demand 5-minute average
The amount of power that is needed on the grid.
Hour-ahead forecast 5-minute average
Power expected to be needed during the day, based on historical system loads and temperatures.
Day-ahead forecast 1-hour average
Power expected to be needed during the day, based on historical system loads and temperatures.
Demand response 5-minute interval
Includes economically dispatched Proxy Demand Resource (PDR) and economically and manually dispatched Reliability Demand Response Resource (RDRR). RDRR may only be manually dispatched if an EEA Watch or EEA 1-3, or Transmission Emergency is issued.
How to use this chart
Data values
Demand: 5-minute average
Hour-ahead forecast: 5-minute average
Day-ahead forecast: 1-hour average
Demand Response: The demand line will display red in color during a significant Demand Response event to indicate that the forecast is diverging from actual demand because of load reduction.
View values
Hover over the chart to view values over a specific time of day.
Hide/Show series
Hide/Show a series by selecting the series in the legend at the bottom of the chart.
Date dropdown
Select a date to see that day’s demand trend data.
Options dropdown
If today’s date: Show forecasted peak and show yesterday’s demand. If historical date: Show today’s demand.
Download dropdown
Export a CSV file based on the date and series selected.

Demand trend data does not include dispatchable pump loads and battery storage that is charging on the system.

Net demand trend information
Net demand
Demand 5-minute average
The amount of power that is needed on the grid.
Net demand 5-minute average
Demand minus wind and solar.
Hour-ahead forecast 5-minute average
Power expected to be needed during the day, based on historical system loads and temperatures.
Day-ahead forecast 1-hour average
Power expected to be needed during the day, based on historical system loads and temperatures.
Day-ahead net forecast 1-hour average
Power expected to be needed during the day minus wind and solar, based on historical system loads and temperatures.
Demand response 5-minute interval
Includes economically dispatched Proxy Demand Resource (PDR) and economically and manually dispatched Reliability Demand Response Resource (RDRR). RDRR may only be manually dispatched if an EEA Watch or EEA 1-3, or Transmission Emergency is issued.
How to use this chart
Data values
Net demand and hour-ahead forecast are 5-minute averages.
Demand Response: The demand line will display red in color during a significant Demand Response event to indicate that the forecast is diverging from actual demand because of load reduction.
View values
Hover over the chart to view values over a specific time of day.
Hide/Show series
Hide/Show a series by selecting the series in the legend at the bottom of the chart.
Date dropdown
Select a date to see that day’s net demand trend data.
Options dropdown
If today’s date: Show forecasted peak and show yesterday’s demand. If historical date: Show today’s demand.
Download dropdown
Export a CSV file based on the date and series selected.

Demand and net demand trend data do not include dispatchable pump loads and battery storage that is charging on the system.

Resource adequacy trend information
Resource adequacy (RA)

This graph helps assess upcoming grid conditions by comparing the forecasted amount of capacity demand compared to the amount contracted under the state’s RA program.

RA capacity1-hour interval
RA is capacity designated by the state to be bid into the market for the reliable operation of the power grid, minus the impacts of outage derates.
Includes data from supplier supply plan, Reliability Must-Run (RMR), and Capacity Procurement Mechanism (CPM).
Net RA capacity1-hour interval
RA capacity minus capacity from wind and solar RA resources.
RA credits
Local regulatory authority credits against the RA obligation for its load serving entities.
Includes data from Demand Response (DR), Cost Allocation Mechanism (CAM), liquidated damages (LD).
Demand 5-minute average
The amount of power that is needed on the grid.
Net demand 5-minute average
Demand minus wind and solar.
Day-ahead forecast 1-hour average
Power expected to be needed during the day, based on historical system loads and temperatures.
Day-ahead net forecast 1-hour average
Power expected to be needed during the day minus wind and solar, based on historical system loads and temperatures.
Reserve requirement 1-hour interval
Amount of reserves required.
Reserve requirement for day-ahead 1-hour interval
1.5% of load for estimated regulation reserve plus 6% of load for estimated contingency reserves.
How to use this chart
View values
Hover over the chart to view values over a specific time of day.
Hide/Show series
Hide/Show a series by selecting the series in the legend at the bottom of the chart.
Date dropdown
Select a date to see that day’s resource adequacy capacity trend data.
Options dropdown
Highlight reserve requirement and/or RA credits.
Download dropdown
Export a CSV file based on the date and series selected.

Demand and net demand trend data do not include dispatchable pump loads and battery storage that is charging on the system.

Resource adequacy (RA)

This graph helps assess upcoming grid conditions by comparing the forecasted amount of capacity demand compared to the amount contracted under the state’s RA program.

Reserve requirement 1-hour interval
Amount of reserves required.
Reserve requirement for day-ahead 1-hour interval
1.5% of load for estimated regulation reserve plus 6% of load for estimated contingency reserves.
How to use this chart
View values
Hover over the chart to view values over a specific time of day.
Hide/Show series
Hide/Show a series by selecting the series in the legend at the bottom of the chart.
Options dropdown
Highlight reserve requirement and/or RA credits.
Download dropdown
Export a CSV file based on the date and series selected.

Demand and net demand trend data do not include dispatchable pump loads and battery storage that is charging on the system.
